The **Basketball Super League** ([Turkish](/wiki/Turkish_language "Turkish language"): *Basketbol Süper Ligi*; **TBSL**),[[1]](#cite_note-1) also known as the **Türkiye Sigorta Basketbol Süper Ligi** for sponsorship reasons, is the top men's [professional basketball](/wiki/Professional_basketball "Professional basketball") division of the [Turkish basketball league system](/wiki/Turkish_basketball_league_system "Turkish basketball league system"). It replaced the former [Turkish Basketball Championship](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_Championship "Turkish Basketball Championship") (1946–1967) to become the **Turkish Basketball League** (TBL) until 2015 when it adopted its current name while the TBL name became exclusive to the [second-tier](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_First_League "Turkish Basketball First League") and [third-tier](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_Second_League "Turkish Basketball Second League") divisions.

The BSL is administered by the [Turkish Basketball Federation](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_Federation "Turkish Basketball Federation") (TBF) and is contested by 16 teams, with the two lowest-placed teams [relegated](/wiki/Promotion_and_relegation "Promotion and relegation") to the second-tier TBL and replaced by the top two teams of that division.

Since the league's restructuring in 1966, 11 clubs have been crowned champions, with [Anadolu Efes](/wiki/Anadolu_Efes_S.K. "Anadolu Efes S.K.") winning the title a record 16 times and [Fenerbahçe](/wiki/Fenerbah%C3%A7e_Basketball "Fenerbahçe Basketball") 12 times. In recent years, Fenerbahçe won 11 titles out of 17 from the [2006–07 season](/wiki/2006%E2%80%9307_Turkish_Basketball_League "2006–07 Turkish Basketball League") onward.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Basketbol_S%C3%BCper_Ligi&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

According to official records, [basketball](/wiki/Basketball "Basketball") was first played in [Turkey](/wiki/Turkey "Turkey") in 1904 at [Robert College](/wiki/Robert_College "Robert College"). An American [physical education](/wiki/Physical_education "Physical education") teacher laid the foundations of the sport in the country. Seven years later, in 1911, [Ahmet Robenson](/wiki/Ahmet_Robenson "Ahmet Robenson"), a physical education teacher at [Galatasaray High School](/wiki/Galatasaray_High_School "Galatasaray High School"), decided to introduce a new game to his students. Robenson, who also later became president of [Galatasaray S.K.](/wiki/Galatasaray_S.K. "Galatasaray S.K."), popularized the sport in Turkey.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Until late 1966, local basketball competitions were held in major cities like [Istanbul](/wiki/Istanbul "Istanbul") (which hosted the [Istanbul League](/wiki/Istanbul_Basketball_League "Istanbul Basketball League")), [Ankara](/wiki/Ankara "Ankara"), and [İzmir](/wiki/%C4%B0zmir "İzmir"). There was also the former [Turkish Championship](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_Championship "Turkish Basketball Championship") which existed from 1946 to 1967.

The current [Turkish top-tier level national league](/wiki/Turkish_basketball_league_system "Turkish basketball league system") was founded in 1966, by the [Turkish Basketball Federation](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_Federation "Turkish Basketball Federation"), and began with the 1966–67 season, and it thus replaced those earlier competitions. The Turkish second-tier level league, the [TBL](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_First_League "Turkish Basketball First League") (previously known as the TB2L), was also founded three years later in 1969, and since 2011, a third-tier level league [TB2L](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_Second_League "Turkish Basketball Second League"), with the teams divided into two groups.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

On 6 March 1988, [Erman Kunter](/wiki/Erman_Kunter "Erman Kunter"), of Fenerbahce Istanbul scored 153 pts against Hilalspor Izmir (92 in 2nd half), a record for a club competition worldwide.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Format

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Basketbol_S%C3%BCper_Ligi&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Format")]

There are 16 teams in the league, and they play against each other twice, under a [league system](/wiki/League_system "League system") format, once at their home and the other away. At the end of the season, the top eight teams are entitled to participate in the league's playoffs. The winner of the playoffs is crowned the Turkish champion. The two top teams of the [Turkish Second League](/wiki/Turkish_Basketball_First_League "Turkish Basketball First League") are [promoted](/wiki/Promotion_and_relegation "Promotion and relegation") to the top level Basketbol Süper Ligi. The two lowest-placed teams of the league are relegated.[[6]](#cite_note-6)
